message:
fix(build-slots): include branch in holder identity to prevent cross-branch slot conflicts

Build slots provide mutual exclusion for cargo builds so that multiple
agents working on the same project don't clobber each other's
compilation artifacts. Previously, holder_identity() ignored the branch
parameter entirely, using only the agent name. This meant that the same
agent working on two different branches (e.g., main vs. a feature
branch) would be treated as the same holder, allowing it to
"re-acquire" a slot it already held on a different branch—defeating
the purpose of branch-aware slot isolation.

Now holder_identity() produces "agent_name:::branch" when a branch is
provided, making each (agent, branch) pair a distinct holder. The
triple-colon separator is intentionally unusual to avoid collisions
with agent names or branch names that might contain single colons.

lease_matches_holder() is also tightened: it now checks both
lease.agent == agent_name AND lease.branch == branch. Previously it
only checked the agent name, which meant an agent on branch A could
incorrectly match a lease held for branch B, allowing concurrent
builds that corrupt the shared target directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
